title: Daphnidium myrrha
taxonomy: Daphnidium myrrha is a genus of flowering plants in the family Thymelaeaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is a member of the clade Angiosperms. The classification of Daphnidium myrrha is as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: Angiosperms, clade: Eudicots, order: Huerteales, family: Thymelaeaceae, genus: Daphnidium, species: D. myrrha. Further classification includes being a monocot or dicot, which in this case is a dicot. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphological features.

side_effects: Daphnidium myrrha is an ancient plant-based medicine, and there is limited information available on its side effects. intro: Daphnidium myrrha, also known as Daphne myrrha, is a plant species belonging to the family Thymelaeaceae, commonly used in traditional medicine and herbalism. The health benefits of Daphnidium myrrha include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as skin infections, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include myrrh resin, which is rich in sesquiterpenes, diterpenes, and triterpenes, including furanosesquiterpenes, diterpenic acids, and triterpenic acids. Daphnidium myrrha is commonly used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, ointments, and salves, as well as being incorporated into traditional perfumes and incense sticks.
